signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

articleMetadata.title: Barcelona swats ALBA aside, 48-101
articleMetadata.description:
U18 FC Barcelona got its Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Ulm campaign off to the best of starts by romping to a dominant 48-101 win against ALBA Berlin in Round 1. 

Sayon Keita did a bit of everything for Barcelona, posting a 24-point, 13-rebound double-double in 21 minutes, as well as 1 assist, 3 steals and 5 blocks.

content.text:
Bologna, Italy, is a city that prides itself on its gastronomic reputation. That's great news for the scores of basketball scouts who will descend there this weekend for the [adidas NextGen EuroLeague](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/) qualifying event. They will be able to eat well and enjoy watching some of the top young basketball talents on the continent.

adidas NextGen Bologna is the third stop of the NextGen EuroLeague qualifying tour with eight teams playing in Bologna from March 13-15. All of the sides are rich in talent and the clubs have each identified a must-see player from their rosters.

Here are those eight players to keep an eye on at the NextGen Bologna event.

 

### Livio Berlingeri, U18 Virtus Bologna

[Livio Berlinger](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/livio-berlingeri/013558/) will be playing his second NextGen event after he posted 5.5 points and 1.0 rebounds in Munich last season. He was part of the Virtus team that won the U20 NextGen Cup with 8.3 points, 2.3 rebounds, 1.7 assists and 1.4 steals - including 7 points in the final. Berlinger is a great athlete who is able to run the floor both with and without the ball. He is a solid one-on-one player, capable of creating solutions at the rim or off the dribble with pull-up jumpers from mid-and three-point range. To take the next step, Berlinger needs to improve his left hand and become more consistent in his spot-up three-point shooting.

 

### Nikola Kusturica, U18 Barcelona

[Nikola Kusturica](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/nikola-kusturica/014128/), who is debuting in the NextGen, is currently averaging 13.1 points, 7.1 rebounds, 1.7 assists and 1.6 steals in the Spanish U22 league. The Serbian has debuted in both the Spanish League and EuroLeague, becoming the youngest Barcelona player to play in the ACB at 16 years, 4 months, and 17 days. Kusturica starred for Serbia in winning the title at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2025 with 20.0 points, 7.7 rebounds, 3.4 assists, 2.1 steals and 1.6 blocks to take home MVP honors. He brings a lot of heart, living basketball with contagious and endless passion. Physically, Kusturica's size and wingspan allow him to cover a lot of ground both defensively and offensively. He is brave attacking the rim and does not hesitate to penetrate. The forward still needs to become more consistent with his shot.

¡SE ESTRENA EN #LigaEndesa! 💥

✨ ¡Nikola Kusturica suma sus primeros puntos en #LigaEndesa!

📺 @DAZN_ES | #LigaEndesa | @FCBbasket pic.twitter.com/AO43ZmssrS

— Liga Endesa (@ACBCOM) February 15, 2026

### Mate Khatiashvili, U18 Fundacion Baskonia Alaves

[Mate Khatiashvili](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/mate-khatiashvili/014106/) is making his debut in the competition. The guard is averaging 14.8 points, 3.5 rebounds, 4.1 assists and 1.4 steals in the Spanish fourth division. Khatiashvili has already debuted with Georgia’s senior national team; he compiled 12 points, 3 rebounds and 3 assists over 37 minutes in four games in the FIBA Basketball World Cup 2027 European Qualifiers. Khatiashvili, who is the youngest to debut for his country, also played twice at the U16 continental level and averaged 17.5 points, 3.8 rebounds, 6.0 assists and 1.8 steals at the FIBA U18 EuroBasket 2025, Division B. Khatiashvili stands out with his competitiveness, though one thing he must improve is his communication on the court.

 

### Cheickh Niang, U18 Dolomiti Energia Trento

[Cheickh Niang](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/cheickh-niang/013756/) will be appearing in the NextGen for a second time after he collected 7.0 points, 4.8 rebounds, 1.2 steals and 1.0 blocks in Belgrade last season. He averaged 6.6 points and 1.7 rebounds in the BKT EuroCup Regular Season this season as well as 3.1 points and 1.5 rebounds in the Italian League. Niang has basketball instincts in his DNA. The younger brother of Virtus Bologna’s Saliou Niang, he is a big-bodied ball handler with athletic and technical skills to attack in different game situations: isolation off the catch, attacking space in motion, finishing at the rim, or shooting from beyond the three-point line. His next step is to become more consistent in using his skills within the team game.

Cheickh Niang makes long range look easy 🎯@AquilaBasketTN | #RoadToGreatness pic.twitter.com/2SFPTXlucI

— BKT EuroCup (@EuroCup) December 16, 2025

### Melvyn Vounang, U18 Paris Basketball

This will be [Melvyn Vounang](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/melvyn-vounang-nanfah/014489/)’s first NextGen tournament. He is averaging 6.1 points, 2.0 rebounds, 1.0 assists and 1.1 steals in the French U21 league and 22 points, 8 rebounds and 2.7 assists in the U18 league. The big man helped France take fifth place at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2025 with 7.1 points, 3.6 rebounds and 1.1 steals. Considered a modern power forward, Vounang stands out with his offensive aggressiveness and his ability to attack the rim with determination. Gifted with real court vision for a big man, Vounang distinguishes himself with his ability to create for others - a true playmaker from the power forward position. He is also capable of spacing the floor and shooting from the perimeter. Vounang is known as a likeable and charismatic player, appreciated for his attitude and genuine work ethic.

 

### Caspar Vossenberg, U18 Bayern Munich

This is [Caspar Vossenberg](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/caspar-vossenberg/013541/)’s second NextGen season after he totalled 12.5 points, 3.0 rebounds, 1.8 assists and 1.5 steals at Munich 2025. He is averaging 5.9 points, 3.4 rebounds and 1.7 assists in the ProB third division as well as 17.8 points, 4.2 rebounds, 1.8 assist and 1.3 steals in the U19 NBBL. Vossenberg helped Bayern win the U19 NBBL crown last season. He can defend multiple positions, slashes against closeouts and can handle the ball in pick-and-roll situations. He also moves well off the ball with good catch-and-shoot capabilities in addition to good athleticism. Vossenberg still needs to work on his rebounding presence, shot creation and more consistent playmaking.

One of the next big guys in German hoops is Caspar Vossenberg of @FCBBasketball
Here is my short look at his path to the #NextGenEuroLeague https://t.co/PNKKzOWUfQ

— David Hein (@heinnews) January 19, 2025

### Ricards Aizpurs, U18 NextGen Team Bologna

[Ricards Aizpurs](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/ricards-aizpurs/013577/) will be playing in his second NextGen event after going for 9.5 points, 2.2 rebounds and 1.5 assists with the Next Gen team in Munich in 2024-25. The Latvian is playing with Stellazzura Rome as well as the Star Alliance, including playing on the Grind Session circuit in the United States. Aizpurs played for Latvia at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024 and averaged 18.1 points, 3.4 rebounds, 2.4 assists, 1.3 steals and 1.1 blocks. He is a very solid all-around player who is physically mature. Aizpurs is a great scorer who can attack teams in various ways.

 

### Ilias Trikkas*, U18 Panathinaikos Athens

[Ilias Trikkas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/ilias-trikkas/014447/) is debuting in the NextGen competition and is averaging 7.6 points, 3.0 rebounds and 3.5 assists in the Rising Stars U18, and 11.3 points, 6.0 rebounds and 1.7 assists in the Greek U18 league. Trikkas started playing basketball with Panathinaikos at age 7. He is a quick point guard who can run the court and open the fast break for his team. He is a good athlete for his size and one of the best rebounders on the team. Trikkas can attack the basket and touch the paint to score or to create for his teammates. He is also a solid shooter.
<center><i>* = Trikkas will miss the tournament due to injury

heroMedia.title: 2016 champion Barcelona, debutant Baskonia headline NextGen Bologna debut
heroMedia.summary: Barcelona enters event with 12-game winning streak in qualifiers

content.text:
The second spot in the 2025-26 Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Finals is up for grabs as Europe's premier youth club competition makes its debut in Bologna, Italy. The NextGen Bologna event is headlined by 2016 continental champion [U18 FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-fc-barcelona/jba/) and the much-anticipated debutant [U18 Fundacion Baskonia-Alaves](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-fundacion-baskonia-alaves/aab/). Eight teams will compete from March 13–15 at the historic PalaDozza, the former home of Virtus Bologna. The first-place finisher will secure a spot in the NextGen Finals at the EuroLeague Final Four in May in Athens.

The other teams in Bologna are [U18 Dolomiti Energia Trento](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-dolomiti-energia-trento/jtn/), [U18 FC Bayern Munich](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-fc-bayern-munich/jmu/), [U18 Next Gen Team Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-next-gen-team-bologna/aaa/), [U18 Panathinaikos Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-panathinaikos-athens/upa/), [U18 Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-paris-basketball/jpb/), and host [U18 Virtus Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-virtus-bologna/jvi/). U18 Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana has already booked the first spot in the Finals after winning the Ulm event. [The NextGen Abu Dhabi was canceled](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/news/adidas-nextgen-euroleague-abu-dhabi-qualifier-cancelled/) during the tournament, and the winner of NextGen Belgrade (March 20-22) will also advance to Athens.

The eight teams are divided into two groups of four, with round-robin play within each group. The top finisher from each group will meet in the first-place game on Sunday, March 15, while the second-place finishers will compete for third place. Positions 5-8 will also be determined. The two groups are as follows: Group A: Barcelona, Bayern, Paris, and Trento; Group B: Baskonia, NGB, Panathinaikos, and Virtus.

### Group A: Barcelona, Bayern, Paris and Trento

Barcelona is one of the most successful teams in the competition's history. The 2016 continental champion also reached the Championship Game in 2008, 2013, and 2021. The Spanish powerhouse is making its 21st appearance and 19th consecutive with a 12-game winning streak in qualifiers – going undefeated in Zadar 2023, Belgrade 2024, and Ulm 2025. Barcelona has reached the NextGen Finals in the last three seasons but hasn't captured the title.

Bayern is competing in the event for the 11th time and fourth consecutive year. The German side also appeared in the NextGen Finals in 2018 and 2019. Bayern finished fifth at home in 2025 with a 2-2 record and was fourth in Podgorica a year earlier with the same mark.

Paris is competing in its second NextGen event after hosting a qualifying tournament in 2024, when it finished seventh with a 1-3 record. Trento is also in the competition for the second time after finishing seventh in Belgrade last season with a 1-3 record.

### Group B: Baskonia, NGB, Panathinaikos and Virtus

Baskonia's debut in the NextGen competition has generated considerable anticipation. The Spanish team has developed strong youth players in the past but is showcasing them at this level for the first time this season.

Next Gen Team Bologna is competing in its fifth season in the event and its second under two-time EuroLeague champion Dogus Balbay as head coach. Long-time Italian international and EuroLeague veteran Massimo Bulleri, who played 126 EuroLeague games, and Kheeryoung Rhee serve as his assistants.

Panathinaikos is in the NextGen for the eighth time and fifth consecutive season. The Greek side finished eighth in Istanbul last season with a 1-3 record after taking sixth at Paris 2024 with the same record. Panathinaikos went 2–2 and finished fifth at Munich 2023, then went 0-3 in the NextGen Finals in Kaunas.

Virtus is competing in the event for the 10th time and third consecutive year. The Italians finished in eighth place winless in Munich last season and went 1-3 for seventh place at 2024 Dubai.

content.text:
Euroleague Basketball regrets to announce the cancellation of the adidas NextGen EuroLeague qualifying tournament in Abu Dhabi.

In light of the current situation in the region, this decision has been made to guarantee the safety and security of all participants. After careful evaluation and ongoing consultation with relevant authorities and stakeholders, it was determined that cancelling the event is the most responsible course of action at this time.

heroMedia.title: adidas NextGen Abu Dhabi: Players to watch
heroMedia.summary: There are a number of exciting players ready to shine in Abu Dhabi

linkedIds.text: U18 Next Gen Team Abu Dhabi

content.text:
The [adidas NextGen EuroLeague](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/) tournament makes its second stop on the qualifying tour as eight teams head to Abu Dhabi from February 27 to March 1. All eight teams feature strong talent, and we asked the clubs to identify their player to watch.

Here are the eight players to watch at [the NextGen Abu Dhabi event](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/news/adidas-nextgen-euroleague-tournament-returns-to-abu-dhabi/).

### Chrysostomos Chatzilamprou (U18 Aris Thessaloniki)

[Chrysostomos Chatzilamprou](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/chrysostomos-chatzilamprou/013046/) will be making his NextGen debut after missing the last two events due to injury. He is averaging 17.9 points, 4.5 rebounds, and 2.4 assists in the U18 Rising Stars League, along with 18.4 points and 5.2 rebounds in the Greek second division. The forward has already made his debut in the Greek League and the BKT EuroCup.

Chatzilamprou starred at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024, posting 17.9 points, 6.0 rebounds, and 1.4 assists per game while earning All-Tournament Team honors. He is a versatile offensive weapon with natural scoring instincts and a unique combination of size, length, and fluidity. His wingspan allows him to impact the boards and finish over smaller guards with ease. He also displays natural leadership and a “big-game” mentality, often demanding the ball in high-leverage situations. Chatzilamprou still needs to improve his defensive consistency and playmaking maturity.

### Tomislav Vuckovic (U18 Dubai Basketball)

[Tomislav Vuckovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/tomislav-vuckovic/013676/) will be playing his second NextGen season after averaging 9.5 points, 2.8 rebounds, and 2.2 steals at the Ulm qualifier, followed by 20.7 points, 2.7 rebounds, and 1.3 steals at the NextGen Finals. At the final event in Abu Dhabi, the Croatian guard set a record for the most three-pointers in a NextGen EuroLeague game with nine in Dubai’s upset of U18 Mega Belgrade. Vuckovic finished that game with 39 points.

Vuckovic is the first player from Dubai’s youth system to debut with the senior team, appearing against Borac Mozzart in the ABA League. He represented Croatia at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024, averaging 14.4 points, 4.1 rebounds, and 3.0 assists. He is an elite shooter and scorer but still needs to improve his consistency and temperament.

content.title: Tomislav Vuckovic pulls up for a jumper at the 2025-26 NextGen Finals.

content.text:
### Andrew Nwokolo (U18 London Lions)

[Andrew Nwokolo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/andrew-nwokolo/014387/) will also be making his NextGen debut in Abu Dhabi. The guard is averaging 9.2 points, 3.8 rebounds, and 1.2 steals for City of London Academy in the British second division, along with 14.4 points and 7.4 rebounds for COLA in Academy Basketball England competition.

Nwokolo played for Great Britain at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024 Division B, averaging 3.3 points and 3.4 rebounds. He is a long, athletic player capable of playing multiple positions. He is a strong finisher who draws contact and earns trips to the free-throw line, and he enjoys finishing above the rim. Defensively, Nwokolo can guard multiple positions thanks to his physical tools.

### Matyss Moudio Martel (U18 AS Monaco)

[Matyss Moudio Martel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/matyss-moudio-martel/013085/) is the only remaining player from Monaco’s previous NextGen appearance at Paris 2023–24, where he averaged 2.3 points and 2.7 rebounds. He is currently averaging 3.8 points, 2.2 rebounds, and 1.2 assists in the French U21 league.

Moudio Martel has excellent hands and shows great versatility on both ends of the floor. He is particularly effective coming off screens.

content.title: Matyss Moudio Martel in action for U18 AS Monaco at the 2023-24 NextGen Paris qualifier.

content.text:
### Omri Orland (U18 NextGen Abu Dhabi)

[Omri Orland](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/omri-orland/014354/) will be making his NextGen debut. He is averaging 11.0 points, 4.0 rebounds, 3.8 assists, and 1.9 steals in Israel’s U18 league. He posted 16.9 points, 7.0 rebounds, 3.9 assists, and 1.9 steals while representing Israel at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2025.

Orland is a talented creator who can handle the ball, shoot, and penetrate. He also has a reliable mid-range jumper and offers strong offensive versatility.

### Javier Viguer (U18 Valencia Basket)

[Javier Viguer](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/javier-viguer/013527/) is competing in NextGen for the second time after averaging 11.8 points, 4.2 rebounds, 1.2 assists, and 1.2 steals while helping Valencia reach the 2024-25 NextGen Munich championship game. He is currently averaging 9.5 points, 3.8 rebounds, and 1.2 assists in the U22 league.

Viguer starred for Spain in its runner-up finish at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024, averaging 12.7 points, 6.0 rebounds, and 2.6 assists. He is a tall, versatile wing with natural offensive instincts and excellent shooting mechanics, capable of knocking down deep three-pointers in high-pressure situations. He shows maturity beyond his years with calm decision-making and the ability to create his own shot against tight defense. With a high basketball IQ, strong footwork, and agility, he can attack the basket efficiently while also stretching the floor. Defensively, he uses his length and anticipation to contest shots and contribute within team schemes.

content.title: U18 Valencia Basket's Javier Viguer shoots a contested shot at the 2024-25 NextGen Munich qualifier.

content.text:
### Egor Amosov (U18 Real Madrid)

[Egor Amosov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/egor-amosov/014118/) will make his NextGen debut while leading his team with 15.2 points per game in the Spanish U22 league, along with 5.9 rebounds, 2.6 assists, and 2.4 steals. His biggest performance came against Valencia in late October, when he scored 42 points, including 8-of-12 from three-point range.

The Russian forward is physically gifted and mature, with exceptional court vision. He is capable of creating in one-on-one situations and uses strong footwork and fakes in the low post. He also punishes defenses in transition and can knock down three-pointers off the dribble. Amosov is versatile and can impact the game from positions one through four. To reach his full potential, he must eliminate defensive lapses and improve his decision-making under pressure, avoiding forced shots and unnecessary one-on-one plays when defenses collapse.

### Ignas Stombergas (U18 Zalgiris Kaunas)

[Ignas Stombergas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/ignas-stombergas/012981/) is playing in NextGen for the second time after playing a major role in Zalgiris winning the title last May, averaging 8.2 points, 5.2 rebounds, and 1.0 blocks at the NextGen Finals. He also averaged 13.5 points, 8.8 rebounds, and 1.2 blocks per game at the Ulm qualifiers.

Stombergas is currently averaging 12.2 points and 7.4 rebounds in Lithuania’s second division (NKL) and has appeared in three games in the Lithuanian League, averaging 3.7 points and 1.0 rebounds. He is an above-the-rim, mobile, rim-running big man who does not rely solely on athleticism. He complements it with a wide low-post skill set, improved perimeter shooting, and a strong defensive presence across the court. Stombergas still needs to improve his strength, overall fluidity in different positions, and outside shooting range.

heroMedia.title: adidas NextGen EuroLeague tournament returns to Abu Dhabi
heroMedia.summary: Europe’s top U18 teams, including champions Zalgiris Kaunas and Real Madrid, to compete February 27–March 1 at Space42 Arena

linkedIds.text: U18 Next Gen Team Abu Dhabi

content.text:
- Official tickets can be purchased at: [https://abu-dhabi.platinumlist.net/event-tickets/105229/adidas-nextgen-euroleague](https://abu-dhabi.platinumlist.net/event-tickets/105229/adidas-nextgen-euroleague)

Abu Dhabi will host the next stop of the 2025–26 adidas NextGen EuroLeague season, bringing eight of Europe’s leading U18 basketball teams to the UAE capital from February 27 to March 1 at the state-of-the-art Space42 Arena. 

Defending continental champion U18 Zalgiris Kaunas and record winner U18 Real Madrid headline the qualifying tournament, with the winner securing a place at the adidas NextGen Finals during the EuroLeague Final Four in Athens, Greece, this May. 

The remaining teams competing in Abu Dhabi are U18 Aris Thessaloniki, U18 AS Monaco, U18 Dubai Basketball, U18 Next Gen Team Abu Dhabi, U18 London Lions and U18 Valencia Basket. U18 Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana has already qualified for the Finals after winning the Ulm event, while the winners of the Bologna (March 13–15) and Belgrade (March 20–22) qualifiers will also advance to Athens.

The eight teams will be divided into two groups of four, playing a round-robin format within their groups. The top teams from each group will meet in the first-place game on Sunday, March 1, while placement games will determine the final standings.

### Group A: Real Madrid joined with Aris, Dubai and Monaco 

Aris is competing in the competition for the third season with the Greek side taking seventh place at NextGen Munich last season with a 2-2 record. Aris was sixth at 1-3 in Dhabi the year before. 

Dubai is also playing in the NextGen for the third campaign. Last season the UAE side went 0-4 and finished eighth at the Ulm qualifier. Abu Dhabi beat U18 Mega Super Belgrade at the NextGen Finals and nearly knocked off U18 EA7 Emporio Armani Milan for a 1-2 mark. Dubai had a 1-3 record as host of a 2024 qualifier and took eighth place. 

Monaco arrives in Abu Dhabi for its second appearance after going winless for eighth place in Paris 2024. Real meanwhile will be looking to bounce back from a disappointing last season, finishing only third at the Munich qualifier - ending Real’s streak of winning consecutive qualifying tournaments at 11. 

Real also had won its last 19 NextGen games dating back to the NextGen Finals in 2022 in Belgrade. Real then lost to Zalgiris in the group stage at the NextGen Finals to miss a chance to make it a three-peat in championships. The Spanish powerhouse is the all-time winner in continental championships with five (2015, 2019, 2021, 2023 and 2024) while also reaching the 2014 Championship Game. This is Real’s 19th consecutive season since 2007-08. 

### Group B: Zalgiris to face London, Next Gen Abu Dhabi and Valencia 

The London Lions make their debut in the competition as the club was playing in the BKT EuroCup for the third time this season. 

The Next Gen Team will be competing in its fifth season in the event and its second with two-time EuroLeague champion Dogus Balbay as head coach. Long-time Italian international and veteran of 126 EuroLeague games Massimo Bulleri and Kheeryoung Rhee are his assistants. 

Valencia Basket is making its 10th NextGen appearance and eighth in a row. The Spanish team twice reached the Finals: Vitoria-Gasteiz in 2019 and as host in 2021. Valencia took second place at the Munich tournament last season after three straight fifth-place finishes the previous three qualifiers. 

Zalgiris for its part has played in every NextGen season with this its 24th appearance. The Lithuanian power won the first-ever event in 2003 and matched that in 2007 before finally taking the crown again last summer in Abu Dhabi. Zalgiris also reached the Championship Game in 2005, 2006 and 2011. The Kaunas team won the Ulm qualifier last season which came after it took second place in its previous three qualifying events.

heroMedia.title: Cedevita Olimpija helping Cameron Houindo take the next step – just like Joan Beringer
heroMedia.summary: The center is following in the footsteps of his fellow compatriot

content.text:
[Cameron Houindo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/cameron-houindo/013063/) showed at the Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Ulm tournament why he is considered one of the top prospects of his generation. He led [U18 Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-cedevita-olimpija-ljubljana/jlj/) to its first-ever championship game, thriving in a development environment where he is following the same path as another French standout.

Houindo was named to the All-Tournament Team after averaging 22.0 points, 7.5 rebounds, 1.2 assists and 1.5 blocks per game, good for an average Performance Index Rating of 26.5. His 33-point performance in the opening game tied a Cedevita club record for points scored in a single NextGen game.

What made the performance even more impressive was that Houindo had not played a game since mid-December before the Ulm event due to a broken bone in his left hand.

“It’s great because it’s been a long time. I was tired of just waiting,” Houindo told David Hein. “I was like, ‘Damn, I need to play basketball.’”

He returned to full-contact practice only on the Monday before the NextGen tournament began.

Prior to the injury, the 2.06-meter center averaged 2.6 points and 1.5 rebounds in eight BKT EuroCup games for Cedevita, along with 3.6 points, 3.0 rebounds and 1.0 blocks in seven Adriatic League (ABA) appearances.

“It’s a blessing. It’s a high level with huge teams and great players,” said Houindo, who turned 18 in January. “I’m grateful to play at Cedevita because they gave me a big opportunity. At my age, I’m lucky to be able to play at this level so much. I’m just trying to learn as much as possible. Even when I make mistakes, I know it’s okay – I’m 18, I have time, and I need those mistakes to get better.”

content.title: Cameron Houindo's top plays at NextGen Ulm
content.description: The best plays of U18 Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana center Cameron Houindo at the 2026 Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Ulm tournament.

content.text:
Houindo was highly regarded well before arriving in Ljubljana. He was named MVP of the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024 after leading France to the title, averaging 13.9 points, 7.9 rebounds, 2.3 steals, and 2.3 blocks per game.

That followed his participation a year younger at the FIBA U17 Basketball World Cup 2024, and then another year-younger appearance at the FIBA U18 EuroBasket 2025, where he averaged 7.6 points, 5.0 rebounds and 1.4 blocks as France finished second.

Houindo also arrived in Ulm with significant NextGen experience, having competed in four tournaments over two seasons with U18 Pole France INSEP Paris. He played at the NextGen Paris and NextGen Finals in 2023-24, followed by the NextGen Belgrade and NextGen Finals last season.

The big man is now trying to pass that experience on to his Cedevita teammates.

“For me, it’s incredible. I try to help my teammates because I’ve played a few EuroCup games, and I try to bring that experience,” Houindo said. “I’m trying to be the best leader I can be, even though I’m not someone who talks a lot.”

When Houindo was considering leaving INSEP, one of the players he spoke with was fellow Frenchman [Joan Beringer](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/beringer-joan/012996/), whom he had faced at the youth level in France. Beringer made headlines last season with his strong performances for Cedevita after leaving France and SIG Strasbourg.

Beringer parlayed that success into being selected 17th overall in the 2025 NBA Draft by the Minnesota Timberwolves.

“What Joan told me – and what really stuck with me – was that you get a lot of individual training,” Houindo said. “I wanted that same experience. I wanted to play a lot, but I also wanted individual work, and that’s what helped me make my decision.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by NextGen EuroLeague (@nextgeneuroleague)

Another major factor was Cedevita’s participation in two high-level competitions each week.

“I thought that was a great opportunity. When I first came to watch a game against Partizan, I remember thinking, ‘That’s where I want to play,’ because the level was so high,” Houindo said. “I really wanted to be part of that to gain experience and improve.”

Houindo feels he has grown both on and off the court.

“On the court, I’m more aggressive now. I’ve learned to play more with the ball and to shoot more,” he said. “Off the court, last year I was at INSEP, which is great for young players – they help you a lot. But I’ve enjoyed being on my own, learning more about myself, and discovering other things through books and basketball.”

Cedevita’s roster also provides Houindo with a valuable mix of players in their prime, veterans and fellow young talents.

“The different generations are really cool because you can see the differences,” Houindo said. “Playing with [Jaka Blazic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/jaka-blazic/003452/) and [Aleksej Nikolic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/aleksej-nikolic/003703/) is incredible. They give me so much experience and teach me how to deal with mental mistakes. Even Mo ([Umoja Gibson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/umoja-gibson/013855/)), [DJ (Stewart)](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/dj-stewart/012700/), and everyone else tries to help me limit mistakes.”

At the same time, 2007-born Turkish talent [Derin Can Ustun](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/derin-can-ustun/012812/) and NextGen teammate [Maks Ciperle](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/ciperle-maks/013858/) are also part of the professional group.

“I’m a pro, but it’s nice to have players your age to play and learn with,” Houindo said. “You don’t feel alone – you know others are at the same stage as you. It builds memories and friendships.”

With his performance in Ulm, Houindo has already given Cedevita fans – and basketball followers across Europe – plenty of lasting memories, with more surely to come.

heroMedia.title: 2016 champion Barcelona, debutant Baskonia headline NextGen Bologna debut
heroMedia.summary: Barcelona enters event with 12-game winning streak in qualifiers

content.text:
The second spot in the 2025-26 Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Finals is up for grabs as Europe's premier youth club competition makes its debut in Bologna, Italy. The NextGen Bologna event is headlined by 2016 continental champion [U18 FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-fc-barcelona/jba/) and the much-anticipated debutant [U18 Fundacion Baskonia-Alaves](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-fundacion-baskonia-alaves/aab/). Eight teams will compete from March 13–15 at the historic PalaDozza, the former home of Virtus Bologna. The first-place finisher will secure a spot in the NextGen Finals at the EuroLeague Final Four in May in Athens.

The other teams in Bologna are [U18 Dolomiti Energia Trento](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-dolomiti-energia-trento/jtn/), [U18 FC Bayern Munich](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-fc-bayern-munich/jmu/), [U18 Next Gen Team Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-next-gen-team-bologna/aaa/), [U18 Panathinaikos Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-panathinaikos-athens/upa/), [U18 Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-paris-basketball/jpb/), and host [U18 Virtus Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-virtus-bologna/jvi/). U18 Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana has already booked the first spot in the Finals after winning the Ulm event. [The NextGen Abu Dhabi was canceled](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/news/adidas-nextgen-euroleague-abu-dhabi-qualifier-cancelled/) during the tournament, and the winner of NextGen Belgrade (March 20-22) will also advance to Athens.

The eight teams are divided into two groups of four, with round-robin play within each group. The top finisher from each group will meet in the first-place game on Sunday, March 15, while the second-place finishers will compete for third place. Positions 5-8 will also be determined. The two groups are as follows: Group A: Barcelona, Bayern, Paris, and Trento; Group B: Baskonia, NGB, Panathinaikos, and Virtus.

### Group A: Barcelona, Bayern, Paris and Trento

Barcelona is one of the most successful teams in the competition's history. The 2016 continental champion also reached the Championship Game in 2008, 2013, and 2021. The Spanish powerhouse is making its 21st appearance and 19th consecutive with a 12-game winning streak in qualifiers – going undefeated in Zadar 2023, Belgrade 2024, and Ulm 2025. Barcelona has reached the NextGen Finals in the last three seasons but hasn't captured the title.

Bayern is competing in the event for the 11th time and fourth consecutive year. The German side also appeared in the NextGen Finals in 2018 and 2019. Bayern finished fifth at home in 2025 with a 2-2 record and was fourth in Podgorica a year earlier with the same mark.

Paris is competing in its second NextGen event after hosting a qualifying tournament in 2024, when it finished seventh with a 1-3 record. Trento is also in the competition for the second time after finishing seventh in Belgrade last season with a 1-3 record.

### Group B: Baskonia, NGB, Panathinaikos and Virtus

Baskonia's debut in the NextGen competition has generated considerable anticipation. The Spanish team has developed strong youth players in the past but is showcasing them at this level for the first time this season.

Next Gen Team Bologna is competing in its fifth season in the event and its second under two-time EuroLeague champion Dogus Balbay as head coach. Long-time Italian international and EuroLeague veteran Massimo Bulleri, who played 126 EuroLeague games, and Kheeryoung Rhee serve as his assistants.

Panathinaikos is in the NextGen for the eighth time and fifth consecutive season. The Greek side finished eighth in Istanbul last season with a 1-3 record after taking sixth at Paris 2024 with the same record. Panathinaikos went 2–2 and finished fifth at Munich 2023, then went 0-3 in the NextGen Finals in Kaunas.

Virtus is competing in the event for the 10th time and third consecutive year. The Italians finished in eighth place winless in Munich last season and went 1-3 for seventh place at 2024 Dubai.

Euroleague Basketball regrets to announce the cancellation of the adidas NextGen EuroLeague qualifying tournament in Abu Dhabi.

In light of the current situation in the region, this decision has been made to guarantee the safety and security of all participants. After careful evaluation and ongoing consultation with relevant authorities and stakeholders, it was determined that cancelling the event is the most responsible course of action at this time.

heroMedia.title: adidas NextGen Abu Dhabi: Players to watch
heroMedia.summary: There are a number of exciting players ready to shine in Abu Dhabi

linkedIds.text: U18 Next Gen Team Abu Dhabi

content.text:
The [adidas NextGen EuroLeague](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/) tournament makes its second stop on the qualifying tour as eight teams head to Abu Dhabi from February 27 to March 1. All eight teams feature strong talent, and we asked the clubs to identify their player to watch.

Here are the eight players to watch at [the NextGen Abu Dhabi event](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/news/adidas-nextgen-euroleague-tournament-returns-to-abu-dhabi/).

### Chrysostomos Chatzilamprou (U18 Aris Thessaloniki)

[Chrysostomos Chatzilamprou](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/chrysostomos-chatzilamprou/013046/) will be making his NextGen debut after missing the last two events due to injury. He is averaging 17.9 points, 4.5 rebounds, and 2.4 assists in the U18 Rising Stars League, along with 18.4 points and 5.2 rebounds in the Greek second division. The forward has already made his debut in the Greek League and the BKT EuroCup.

Chatzilamprou starred at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024, posting 17.9 points, 6.0 rebounds, and 1.4 assists per game while earning All-Tournament Team honors. He is a versatile offensive weapon with natural scoring instincts and a unique combination of size, length, and fluidity. His wingspan allows him to impact the boards and finish over smaller guards with ease. He also displays natural leadership and a “big-game” mentality, often demanding the ball in high-leverage situations. Chatzilamprou still needs to improve his defensive consistency and playmaking maturity.

### Tomislav Vuckovic (U18 Dubai Basketball)

[Tomislav Vuckovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/tomislav-vuckovic/013676/) will be playing his second NextGen season after averaging 9.5 points, 2.8 rebounds, and 2.2 steals at the Ulm qualifier, followed by 20.7 points, 2.7 rebounds, and 1.3 steals at the NextGen Finals. At the final event in Abu Dhabi, the Croatian guard set a record for the most three-pointers in a NextGen EuroLeague game with nine in Dubai’s upset of U18 Mega Belgrade. Vuckovic finished that game with 39 points.

Vuckovic is the first player from Dubai’s youth system to debut with the senior team, appearing against Borac Mozzart in the ABA League. He represented Croatia at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024, averaging 14.4 points, 4.1 rebounds, and 3.0 assists. He is an elite shooter and scorer but still needs to improve his consistency and temperament.

content.title: Tomislav Vuckovic pulls up for a jumper at the 2025-26 NextGen Finals.

content.text:
### Andrew Nwokolo (U18 London Lions)

[Andrew Nwokolo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/andrew-nwokolo/014387/) will also be making his NextGen debut in Abu Dhabi. The guard is averaging 9.2 points, 3.8 rebounds, and 1.2 steals for City of London Academy in the British second division, along with 14.4 points and 7.4 rebounds for COLA in Academy Basketball England competition.

Nwokolo played for Great Britain at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024 Division B, averaging 3.3 points and 3.4 rebounds. He is a long, athletic player capable of playing multiple positions. He is a strong finisher who draws contact and earns trips to the free-throw line, and he enjoys finishing above the rim. Defensively, Nwokolo can guard multiple positions thanks to his physical tools.

### Matyss Moudio Martel (U18 AS Monaco)

[Matyss Moudio Martel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/matyss-moudio-martel/013085/) is the only remaining player from Monaco’s previous NextGen appearance at Paris 2023–24, where he averaged 2.3 points and 2.7 rebounds. He is currently averaging 3.8 points, 2.2 rebounds, and 1.2 assists in the French U21 league.

Moudio Martel has excellent hands and shows great versatility on both ends of the floor. He is particularly effective coming off screens.

content.title: Matyss Moudio Martel in action for U18 AS Monaco at the 2023-24 NextGen Paris qualifier.

content.text:
### Omri Orland (U18 NextGen Abu Dhabi)

[Omri Orland](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/omri-orland/014354/) will be making his NextGen debut. He is averaging 11.0 points, 4.0 rebounds, 3.8 assists, and 1.9 steals in Israel’s U18 league. He posted 16.9 points, 7.0 rebounds, 3.9 assists, and 1.9 steals while representing Israel at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2025.

Orland is a talented creator who can handle the ball, shoot, and penetrate. He also has a reliable mid-range jumper and offers strong offensive versatility.

### Javier Viguer (U18 Valencia Basket)

[Javier Viguer](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/javier-viguer/013527/) is competing in NextGen for the second time after averaging 11.8 points, 4.2 rebounds, 1.2 assists, and 1.2 steals while helping Valencia reach the 2024-25 NextGen Munich championship game. He is currently averaging 9.5 points, 3.8 rebounds, and 1.2 assists in the U22 league.

Viguer starred for Spain in its runner-up finish at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024, averaging 12.7 points, 6.0 rebounds, and 2.6 assists. He is a tall, versatile wing with natural offensive instincts and excellent shooting mechanics, capable of knocking down deep three-pointers in high-pressure situations. He shows maturity beyond his years with calm decision-making and the ability to create his own shot against tight defense. With a high basketball IQ, strong footwork, and agility, he can attack the basket efficiently while also stretching the floor. Defensively, he uses his length and anticipation to contest shots and contribute within team schemes.

content.title: U18 Valencia Basket's Javier Viguer shoots a contested shot at the 2024-25 NextGen Munich qualifier.

content.text:
### Egor Amosov (U18 Real Madrid)

[Egor Amosov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/egor-amosov/014118/) will make his NextGen debut while leading his team with 15.2 points per game in the Spanish U22 league, along with 5.9 rebounds, 2.6 assists, and 2.4 steals. His biggest performance came against Valencia in late October, when he scored 42 points, including 8-of-12 from three-point range.

The Russian forward is physically gifted and mature, with exceptional court vision. He is capable of creating in one-on-one situations and uses strong footwork and fakes in the low post. He also punishes defenses in transition and can knock down three-pointers off the dribble. Amosov is versatile and can impact the game from positions one through four. To reach his full potential, he must eliminate defensive lapses and improve his decision-making under pressure, avoiding forced shots and unnecessary one-on-one plays when defenses collapse.

### Ignas Stombergas (U18 Zalgiris Kaunas)

[Ignas Stombergas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/ignas-stombergas/012981/) is playing in NextGen for the second time after playing a major role in Zalgiris winning the title last May, averaging 8.2 points, 5.2 rebounds, and 1.0 blocks at the NextGen Finals. He also averaged 13.5 points, 8.8 rebounds, and 1.2 blocks per game at the Ulm qualifiers.

Stombergas is currently averaging 12.2 points and 7.4 rebounds in Lithuania’s second division (NKL) and has appeared in three games in the Lithuanian League, averaging 3.7 points and 1.0 rebounds. He is an above-the-rim, mobile, rim-running big man who does not rely solely on athleticism. He complements it with a wide low-post skill set, improved perimeter shooting, and a strong defensive presence across the court. Stombergas still needs to improve his strength, overall fluidity in different positions, and outside shooting range.

heroMedia.summary: Europe’s top U18 teams, including champions Zalgiris Kaunas and Real Madrid, to compete February 27–March 1 at Space42 Arena

linkedIds.text: U18 Next Gen Team Abu Dhabi

content.text:
- Official tickets can be purchased at: [https://abu-dhabi.platinumlist.net/event-tickets/105229/adidas-nextgen-euroleague](https://abu-dhabi.platinumlist.net/event-tickets/105229/adidas-nextgen-euroleague)

Abu Dhabi will host the next stop of the 2025–26 adidas NextGen EuroLeague season, bringing eight of Europe’s leading U18 basketball teams to the UAE capital from February 27 to March 1 at the state-of-the-art Space42 Arena. 

Defending continental champion U18 Zalgiris Kaunas and record winner U18 Real Madrid headline the qualifying tournament, with the winner securing a place at the adidas NextGen Finals during the EuroLeague Final Four in Athens, Greece, this May. 

The remaining teams competing in Abu Dhabi are U18 Aris Thessaloniki, U18 AS Monaco, U18 Dubai Basketball, U18 Next Gen Team Abu Dhabi, U18 London Lions and U18 Valencia Basket. U18 Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana has already qualified for the Finals after winning the Ulm event, while the winners of the Bologna (March 13–15) and Belgrade (March 20–22) qualifiers will also advance to Athens.

The eight teams will be divided into two groups of four, playing a round-robin format within their groups. The top teams from each group will meet in the first-place game on Sunday, March 1, while placement games will determine the final standings.

### Group A: Real Madrid joined with Aris, Dubai and Monaco 

Aris is competing in the competition for the third season with the Greek side taking seventh place at NextGen Munich last season with a 2-2 record. Aris was sixth at 1-3 in Dhabi the year before. 

Dubai is also playing in the NextGen for the third campaign. Last season the UAE side went 0-4 and finished eighth at the Ulm qualifier. Abu Dhabi beat U18 Mega Super Belgrade at the NextGen Finals and nearly knocked off U18 EA7 Emporio Armani Milan for a 1-2 mark. Dubai had a 1-3 record as host of a 2024 qualifier and took eighth place. 

Monaco arrives in Abu Dhabi for its second appearance after going winless for eighth place in Paris 2024. Real meanwhile will be looking to bounce back from a disappointing last season, finishing only third at the Munich qualifier - ending Real’s streak of winning consecutive qualifying tournaments at 11. 

Real also had won its last 19 NextGen games dating back to the NextGen Finals in 2022 in Belgrade. Real then lost to Zalgiris in the group stage at the NextGen Finals to miss a chance to make it a three-peat in championships. The Spanish powerhouse is the all-time winner in continental championships with five (2015, 2019, 2021, 2023 and 2024) while also reaching the 2014 Championship Game. This is Real’s 19th consecutive season since 2007-08. 

### Group B: Zalgiris to face London, Next Gen Abu Dhabi and Valencia 

The London Lions make their debut in the competition as the club was playing in the BKT EuroCup for the third time this season. 

The Next Gen Team will be competing in its fifth season in the event and its second with two-time EuroLeague champion Dogus Balbay as head coach. Long-time Italian international and veteran of 126 EuroLeague games Massimo Bulleri and Kheeryoung Rhee are his assistants. 

Valencia Basket is making its 10th NextGen appearance and eighth in a row. The Spanish team twice reached the Finals: Vitoria-Gasteiz in 2019 and as host in 2021. Valencia took second place at the Munich tournament last season after three straight fifth-place finishes the previous three qualifiers. 

Zalgiris for its part has played in every NextGen season with this its 24th appearance. The Lithuanian power won the first-ever event in 2003 and matched that in 2007 before finally taking the crown again last summer in Abu Dhabi. Zalgiris also reached the Championship Game in 2005, 2006 and 2011. The Kaunas team won the Ulm qualifier last season which came after it took second place in its previous three qualifying events.

heroMedia.title: Cedevita Olimpija helping Cameron Houindo take the next step – just like Joan Beringer
heroMedia.summary: The center is following in the footsteps of his fellow compatriot

content.text:
[Cameron Houindo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/cameron-houindo/013063/) showed at the Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Ulm tournament why he is considered one of the top prospects of his generation. He led [U18 Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/teams/u18-cedevita-olimpija-ljubljana/jlj/) to its first-ever championship game, thriving in a development environment where he is following the same path as another French standout.

Houindo was named to the All-Tournament Team after averaging 22.0 points, 7.5 rebounds, 1.2 assists and 1.5 blocks per game, good for an average Performance Index Rating of 26.5. His 33-point performance in the opening game tied a Cedevita club record for points scored in a single NextGen game.

What made the performance even more impressive was that Houindo had not played a game since mid-December before the Ulm event due to a broken bone in his left hand.

“It’s great because it’s been a long time. I was tired of just waiting,” Houindo told David Hein. “I was like, ‘Damn, I need to play basketball.’”

He returned to full-contact practice only on the Monday before the NextGen tournament began.

Prior to the injury, the 2.06-meter center averaged 2.6 points and 1.5 rebounds in eight BKT EuroCup games for Cedevita, along with 3.6 points, 3.0 rebounds and 1.0 blocks in seven Adriatic League (ABA) appearances.

“It’s a blessing. It’s a high level with huge teams and great players,” said Houindo, who turned 18 in January. “I’m grateful to play at Cedevita because they gave me a big opportunity. At my age, I’m lucky to be able to play at this level so much. I’m just trying to learn as much as possible. Even when I make mistakes, I know it’s okay – I’m 18, I have time, and I need those mistakes to get better.”

content.title: Cameron Houindo's top plays at NextGen Ulm
content.description: The best plays of U18 Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana center Cameron Houindo at the 2026 Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Ulm tournament.

content.text:
Houindo was highly regarded well before arriving in Ljubljana. He was named MVP of the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024 after leading France to the title, averaging 13.9 points, 7.9 rebounds, 2.3 steals, and 2.3 blocks per game.

That followed his participation a year younger at the FIBA U17 Basketball World Cup 2024, and then another year-younger appearance at the FIBA U18 EuroBasket 2025, where he averaged 7.6 points, 5.0 rebounds and 1.4 blocks as France finished second.

Houindo also arrived in Ulm with significant NextGen experience, having competed in four tournaments over two seasons with U18 Pole France INSEP Paris. He played at the NextGen Paris and NextGen Finals in 2023-24, followed by the NextGen Belgrade and NextGen Finals last season.

The big man is now trying to pass that experience on to his Cedevita teammates.

“For me, it’s incredible. I try to help my teammates because I’ve played a few EuroCup games, and I try to bring that experience,” Houindo said. “I’m trying to be the best leader I can be, even though I’m not someone who talks a lot.”

When Houindo was considering leaving INSEP, one of the players he spoke with was fellow Frenchman [Joan Beringer](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/beringer-joan/012996/), whom he had faced at the youth level in France. Beringer made headlines last season with his strong performances for Cedevita after leaving France and SIG Strasbourg.

Beringer parlayed that success into being selected 17th overall in the 2025 NBA Draft by the Minnesota Timberwolves.

“What Joan told me – and what really stuck with me – was that you get a lot of individual training,” Houindo said. “I wanted that same experience. I wanted to play a lot, but I also wanted individual work, and that’s what helped me make my decision.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by NextGen EuroLeague (@nextgeneuroleague)

Another major factor was Cedevita’s participation in two high-level competitions each week.

“I thought that was a great opportunity. When I first came to watch a game against Partizan, I remember thinking, ‘That’s where I want to play,’ because the level was so high,” Houindo said. “I really wanted to be part of that to gain experience and improve.”

Houindo feels he has grown both on and off the court.

“On the court, I’m more aggressive now. I’ve learned to play more with the ball and to shoot more,” he said. “Off the court, last year I was at INSEP, which is great for young players – they help you a lot. But I’ve enjoyed being on my own, learning more about myself, and discovering other things through books and basketball.”

Cedevita’s roster also provides Houindo with a valuable mix of players in their prime, veterans and fellow young talents.

“The different generations are really cool because you can see the differences,” Houindo said. “Playing with [Jaka Blazic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/jaka-blazic/003452/) and [Aleksej Nikolic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/aleksej-nikolic/003703/) is incredible. They give me so much experience and teach me how to deal with mental mistakes. Even Mo ([Umoja Gibson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/umoja-gibson/013855/)), [DJ (Stewart)](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/dj-stewart/012700/), and everyone else tries to help me limit mistakes.”

At the same time, 2007-born Turkish talent [Derin Can Ustun](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/derin-can-ustun/012812/) and NextGen teammate [Maks Ciperle](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/ciperle-maks/013858/) are also part of the professional group.

“I’m a pro, but it’s nice to have players your age to play and learn with,” Houindo said. “You don’t feel alone – you know others are at the same stage as you. It builds memories and friendships.”

With his performance in Ulm, Houindo has already given Cedevita fans – and basketball followers across Europe – plenty of lasting memories, with more surely to come.

content.text:
Bologna, Italy, is a city that prides itself on its gastronomic reputation. That's great news for the scores of basketball scouts who will descend there this weekend for the [adidas NextGen EuroLeague](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/) qualifying event. They will be able to eat well and enjoy watching some of the top young basketball talents on the continent.

adidas NextGen Bologna is the third stop of the NextGen EuroLeague qualifying tour with eight teams playing in Bologna from March 13-15. All of the sides are rich in talent and the clubs have each identified a must-see player from their rosters.

Here are those eight players to keep an eye on at the NextGen Bologna event.

 

### Livio Berlingeri, U18 Virtus Bologna

[Livio Berlinger](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/livio-berlingeri/013558/) will be playing his second NextGen event after he posted 5.5 points and 1.0 rebounds in Munich last season. He was part of the Virtus team that won the U20 NextGen Cup with 8.3 points, 2.3 rebounds, 1.7 assists and 1.4 steals - including 7 points in the final. Berlinger is a great athlete who is able to run the floor both with and without the ball. He is a solid one-on-one player, capable of creating solutions at the rim or off the dribble with pull-up jumpers from mid-and three-point range. To take the next step, Berlinger needs to improve his left hand and become more consistent in his spot-up three-point shooting.

 

### Nikola Kusturica, U18 Barcelona

[Nikola Kusturica](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/nikola-kusturica/014128/), who is debuting in the NextGen, is currently averaging 13.1 points, 7.1 rebounds, 1.7 assists and 1.6 steals in the Spanish U22 league. The Serbian has debuted in both the Spanish League and EuroLeague, becoming the youngest Barcelona player to play in the ACB at 16 years, 4 months, and 17 days. Kusturica starred for Serbia in winning the title at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2025 with 20.0 points, 7.7 rebounds, 3.4 assists, 2.1 steals and 1.6 blocks to take home MVP honors. He brings a lot of heart, living basketball with contagious and endless passion. Physically, Kusturica's size and wingspan allow him to cover a lot of ground both defensively and offensively. He is brave attacking the rim and does not hesitate to penetrate. The forward still needs to become more consistent with his shot.

¡SE ESTRENA EN #LigaEndesa! 💥

✨ ¡Nikola Kusturica suma sus primeros puntos en #LigaEndesa!

📺 @DAZN_ES | #LigaEndesa | @FCBbasket pic.twitter.com/AO43ZmssrS

— Liga Endesa (@ACBCOM) February 15, 2026

### Mate Khatiashvili, U18 Fundacion Baskonia Alaves

[Mate Khatiashvili](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/mate-khatiashvili/014106/) is making his debut in the competition. The guard is averaging 14.8 points, 3.5 rebounds, 4.1 assists and 1.4 steals in the Spanish fourth division. Khatiashvili has already debuted with Georgia’s senior national team; he compiled 12 points, 3 rebounds and 3 assists over 37 minutes in four games in the FIBA Basketball World Cup 2027 European Qualifiers. Khatiashvili, who is the youngest to debut for his country, also played twice at the U16 continental level and averaged 17.5 points, 3.8 rebounds, 6.0 assists and 1.8 steals at the FIBA U18 EuroBasket 2025, Division B. Khatiashvili stands out with his competitiveness, though one thing he must improve is his communication on the court.

 

### Cheickh Niang, U18 Dolomiti Energia Trento

[Cheickh Niang](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/cheickh-niang/013756/) will be appearing in the NextGen for a second time after he collected 7.0 points, 4.8 rebounds, 1.2 steals and 1.0 blocks in Belgrade last season. He averaged 6.6 points and 1.7 rebounds in the BKT EuroCup Regular Season this season as well as 3.1 points and 1.5 rebounds in the Italian League. Niang has basketball instincts in his DNA. The younger brother of Virtus Bologna’s Saliou Niang, he is a big-bodied ball handler with athletic and technical skills to attack in different game situations: isolation off the catch, attacking space in motion, finishing at the rim, or shooting from beyond the three-point line. His next step is to become more consistent in using his skills within the team game.

Cheickh Niang makes long range look easy 🎯@AquilaBasketTN | #RoadToGreatness pic.twitter.com/2SFPTXlucI

— BKT EuroCup (@EuroCup) December 16, 2025

### Melvyn Vounang, U18 Paris Basketball

This will be [Melvyn Vounang](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/melvyn-vounang-nanfah/014489/)’s first NextGen tournament. He is averaging 6.1 points, 2.0 rebounds, 1.0 assists and 1.1 steals in the French U21 league and 22 points, 8 rebounds and 2.7 assists in the U18 league. The big man helped France take fifth place at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2025 with 7.1 points, 3.6 rebounds and 1.1 steals. Considered a modern power forward, Vounang stands out with his offensive aggressiveness and his ability to attack the rim with determination. Gifted with real court vision for a big man, Vounang distinguishes himself with his ability to create for others - a true playmaker from the power forward position. He is also capable of spacing the floor and shooting from the perimeter. Vounang is known as a likeable and charismatic player, appreciated for his attitude and genuine work ethic.

 

### Caspar Vossenberg, U18 Bayern Munich

This is [Caspar Vossenberg](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/caspar-vossenberg/013541/)’s second NextGen season after he totalled 12.5 points, 3.0 rebounds, 1.8 assists and 1.5 steals at Munich 2025. He is averaging 5.9 points, 3.4 rebounds and 1.7 assists in the ProB third division as well as 17.8 points, 4.2 rebounds, 1.8 assist and 1.3 steals in the U19 NBBL. Vossenberg helped Bayern win the U19 NBBL crown last season. He can defend multiple positions, slashes against closeouts and can handle the ball in pick-and-roll situations. He also moves well off the ball with good catch-and-shoot capabilities in addition to good athleticism. Vossenberg still needs to work on his rebounding presence, shot creation and more consistent playmaking.

One of the next big guys in German hoops is Caspar Vossenberg of @FCBBasketball
Here is my short look at his path to the #NextGenEuroLeague https://t.co/PNKKzOWUfQ

— David Hein (@heinnews) January 19, 2025

### Ricards Aizpurs, U18 NextGen Team Bologna

[Ricards Aizpurs](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/ricards-aizpurs/013577/) will be playing in his second NextGen event after going for 9.5 points, 2.2 rebounds and 1.5 assists with the Next Gen team in Munich in 2024-25. The Latvian is playing with Stellazzura Rome as well as the Star Alliance, including playing on the Grind Session circuit in the United States. Aizpurs played for Latvia at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024 and averaged 18.1 points, 3.4 rebounds, 2.4 assists, 1.3 steals and 1.1 blocks. He is a very solid all-around player who is physically mature. Aizpurs is a great scorer who can attack teams in various ways.

 

### Ilias Trikkas*, U18 Panathinaikos Athens

[Ilias Trikkas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/ilias-trikkas/014447/) is debuting in the NextGen competition and is averaging 7.6 points, 3.0 rebounds and 3.5 assists in the Rising Stars U18, and 11.3 points, 6.0 rebounds and 1.7 assists in the Greek U18 league. Trikkas started playing basketball with Panathinaikos at age 7. He is a quick point guard who can run the court and open the fast break for his team. He is a good athlete for his size and one of the best rebounders on the team. Trikkas can attack the basket and touch the paint to score or to create for his teammates. He is also a solid shooter.
<center><i>* = Trikkas will miss the tournament due to injury

heroMedia.title: Mathieu Grujicic only needed 5 minutes to decide to join Barcelona
heroMedia.summary: The German-French forward has taken many steps forward since joining the Spanish club

content.text:
As far as European basketball clubs go, FC Barcelona is one of the best on the continent. So it should be no surprise that [Mathieu Grujicic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/ngt/players/mathieu-grujicic/012103/) jumped at the chance to leave Germany and join the Spanish powerhouse. And now he helped the club’s U18 team finish first at the Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Ulm, being named winner of the [Most Valuable Player](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/ngt/news/mathieu-grujicic-honored-as-nextgen-ulm-mvp-after-barcelona-title/) award.

Grujicic tallied 22 points, 2 rebounds, 1 assist and 1 steal drew 8 fouls for a performance index rating of 20 in Barcelona’s 91-89 win over U18 Zalgiris Kaunas in the first-place game. For the tournament, Grujicic averaged 18.5 points on 47.4% shooting from the outside, 2.5 rebounds, 2.0 assists and 2.2 steals in collecting a PIR of 19.8.

“I couldn't have done it without my teammates. I think I stepped it up in the second half against Zalgiris and we kept working,” said Grujicic, who finished the tournament third in scoring and fifth in PIR.

Crowned in 🇩🇪

Mathieu Grujicic is the Adidas #NextGenEuroLeague Ulm Qualifier MVP⭐️#adidasbasketball pic.twitter.com/tfdfkcd1aN

— Turkish Airlines EuroLeague (@EuroLeague) February 9, 2025

Barcelona head coach Carlos Marin praised the player he sees as one of his leaders.

“He’s able to produce in a lot of ways. He can shoot threes off the dribble, off screens, one-on-one situations, he can share the ball, he can create assists. He has really improved his defense a lot too. It’s great to have him,” said the coach, who is spending his second season with the versatile wing.

Marin made the jump from Barcelona youth assistant to head coach just as Grujicic had come to the club. Grujicic was born in Arles, France - located between Montpellier and Marseille in the south of the country. His father is Serbian and mother is French and they moved to Berlin in 2012 because his mother was working in the French embassy in the German capital. 

Grujicic’s first NextGen experience came in 2022-23 with U18 ALBA Berlin in Munich. Later that season, Grujicic helped ALBA win the German U16 JBBL league title with 52 points, 9 rebounds, 4 assists and 7 steals in 46 minutes of a 93-85 double-OT victory over Eintracht Frankfurt / Fraport Skyliners in the Final to take home Final Four MVP.

In the summer, Grujicic averaged 14.9 points, 5.7 rebounds and 2.4 steals in helping Germany finish fifth at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2023 to help the country qualify for the FIBA U17 World Cup for the first time.
"Barcelona is without a doubt the biggest club in Spain and probably in Europe. So for me, it wasn't a hard decision at all."
content.author: Mathieu Grujicic

content.text:
Then in September 2023, it was time to decide on his future.

“When I found out I had the opportunity to go to Barca, I had 24 hours to think about it. And I remember exactly that after five minutes, I called and I said, 'No, for me, there's nothing to think about.' Because that's something that I've always wanted to do: play in Spain, play at one of the big clubs in Spain. And Barcelona is without a doubt the biggest club in Spain and probably in Europe. So for me, it wasn't a hard decision at all,” Grujicic recalled.

Since moving to Spain, Grujicic feels he has progressed both on and off the court.

“I really think I developed as a player, but also as a human. I perfected an additional language. I met so many new friends and new people that now I can call my family,” said Grujicic, who speaks fluent French, German, English and Spanish and added: “My Serbo-Croat can get a little better.”

Grujicic then last season helped Barcelona win the NextGen Belgrade qualifying tournament while averaging 15.2 points, 3.0 rebounds and 1.5 assists. The team returned to the NextGen Finals in Berlin and opened the tournament with an emphatic 118-46 win over ALBA with Grujicic picking up 8 points against his former team.

content.title: Mathieu Grujicic highlights from NextGen EuroLeague Ulm
content.description: Mathieu Grujicic highlights from NextGen EuroLeague Ulm

content.text:
In the second game, Barcelona wasted a 14-point lead and ended up losing 102-98 to U18 Overtime Elite. And then Grujicic and co. could not stop Nolan Traore in the third game as the U18 CFBB INSEP Paris star scored a NextGen Finals record 45 points to go with 6 rebounds and 9 assists for an index rating of 50 in INSEP’s 101-96 victory.

This season, Grujicic is one of the main leaders of Barcelona’s second team in the fourth division with 14.0 points, 5.8 rebounds and 2.2 assists. And he helped Barcelona show why it was considered one of the favorites in Ulm.

“We came here to win this thing, not just to participate. And in the end, we did it. We knew it wasn't going to be an easy game, there aren’t any easy games. Every game is difficult. But we stuck to our game plan,” he said.

Now Grujicic is looking forward to his first trip to Abu Dhabi where he knows the challenge will be even greater at the NextGen Finals.

“These Finals are always tough to really have a favorite. There are no bad teams, they're all the best teams in Europe. So I wouldn't really call us a favorite. I would call us one of the favorites,” he said.

Barcelona has been waiting for some time to finally hoist its second NextGen EuroLeague trophy - with the only one in club history coming in 2016.

“That would be amazing, man. I would be so happy if I could make it happen for my teammates, for the coaches. That would be an amazing accomplishment for our head coach. In his second year already, two Finals appearances and one championship. We're just trying to make him proud, the whole staff. So yeah, it would mean a lot.”

And it would be another confirmation of why it was an easy decision to move to Barcelona back in 2023.

heroMedia.title: 28 elite U18 teams to compete in 2026 Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Qualifiers
heroMedia.summary: Four qualifying tournaments will feature teams from 12 countries

content.text:
The 2026 [Adidas NextGen EuroLeague](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/) qualifying phase will feature 28 of the top under-18 programs from across Europe and beyond, competing in four three-day tournaments between February and March.

Teams representing 12 countries will take part in the qualifying events as they pursue a place in the season-ending NextGen EuroLeague Finals, where the continental NextGen Club Champion will be crowned alongside the EuroLeague Final Four.

Nineteen of the participating teams are youth teams of EuroLeague clubs, while six represent BKT EuroCup organizations. Three additional teams have been invited, and each tournament will also include an adidas Next Generation Team, featuring elite players from non‑qualifying clubs.

### Ulm Qualifying Tournament (February 6-8)

The first qualifying tournament will be hosted by ratiopharm ulm in Ulm. The participants are:

- Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana
- EA7 Emporio Armani Milan
- LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne
- Mega Super Belgrade
- NextGen Team Ulm
- Occident Basket Manresa
- Olympiacos Piraeus

### Abu Dhabi Qualifying Tournament (February 27-March 1)

The second event will be held in Abu Dhabi and will feature:

- Aris Thessaloniki
- AS Monaco
- Dubai Basketball
- NextGen Team Abu Dhabi
- London Lions
- Real Madrid
- Valencia Basket
- Zalgiris Kaunas

### Bologna Qualifying Tournament (March 13-15)

The third qualifying tournament will take place in Bologna, hosted by Virtus Bologna, with the following teams in the field:

- Dolomiti Energia Trento
- FC Barcelona
- FC Bayern Munich
- Fundacion Baskonia-Alaves
- NextGen Team Bologna
- Panathinaikos Athens
- Paris Basketball

### Belgrade Qualifying Tournament (March 20-22)

The fourth and final qualifying tournament will be staged in Belgrade and hosted by Crvena Zvezda Belgrade. Competing alongside the hosts will be:

- Anadolu Efes Istanbul
- FMP Belgrade
- Hapoel Tel Aviv
- Maccabi Tel Aviv
- NextGen Team Belgrade
- Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade
- Pole France INSEP Paris

Across the four tournaments, teams from Spain (5), Serbia (4), France (4), Greece (3), Italy (3), Germany (2), Israel (2), Lithuania (1), Slovenia (1), Turkiye (1), the United Arab Emirates (1), and the United Kingdom (1) will be represented.

The qualifying tournaments will determine the teams advancing to the NextGen EuroLeague Finals, completing the field for the competition’s championship phase.

heroMedia.title: 2025-26 Adidas NextGen EuroLeague returns to spotlight Europe’s top prospects
heroMedia.summary: Ulm, Abu Dhabi, Bologna and Belgrade will host the Qualifiers

content.text:
The [Adidas NextGen EuroLeague](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/) returns for the 2025–26 season, carrying forward the momentum of last year’s refreshed identity. Now entering its 24th edition, the competition continues to reinforce its place within the Euroleague Basketball framework and its reputation as the leading stage for Europe’s top U18 prospects, many of whom go on to become central figures in the senior game.

As always, the season will be built around four Qualifying Tournaments held between late autumn and early spring. The winner of each event will book a spot at the 2026 Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Finals, which will once again take place during the EuroLeague Final Four in Athens, Greece, creating a shared spotlight for both emerging and established stars.

The Next Generation Team, made up of standout players from clubs not represented in the qualifiers, will continue to feature at every stop on the calendar. Dogus Balbay, a 2021 and 2022 EuroLeague champion, will coach the team for the second consecutive season. Balbay made an impressive debut last year, guiding the U18 Next Generation Team to the Istanbul Qualifier Final and winning the Munich Qualifier. The 2025-26 Qualifying Tournaments:

### Ulm, Germany – February 6-8, 2026

The season begins with the opening qualifier hosted at Orange Campus, which is renowned across Europe for its commitment to youth development.

### Abu Dhabi, UAE – February 27-March 1, 2026

The action then moves to the second stop, where teams will compete at the state-of-the-art Space42 Arena. This marks the third occasion that the NextGen EuroLeague will be held in the Middle East, after Dubai hosted a qualifier in 2024 and Abu Dhabi staged last season’s Finals.

### Bologna, Italy – March 13-15, 2026

In mid-March, the competition continues at the historic PalaDozza, the former home of Virtus Bologna for many years. Bologna will make its debut as a NextGen host, bringing all the excitement of the tournament to a growing Italian fan base.

### Belgrade, Serbia – March 20-22, 2026

Belgrade, a familiar and longstanding stop on the NextGen circuit, will host the final qualifier at Aleksandar Nikolic Hall, with Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade taking on hosting duties.

Over the years, the competition has seen many of today’s biggest names pass through its ranks, including [Luka Doncic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/players/luka-doncic/005929/), [Nikola Jokic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/nikola-jokic/004740/), [Victor Wembanyama](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/wembanyama-victor/009553/), [Kristaps Porzingis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/kristaps-porzingis/003695/), [Domantas Sabonis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/domantas-sabonis/004485/), [Milos Teodosic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/milos-teodosic/jdr/), [Vasilije Micic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/vasilije-micic/002580/), [Nikola Mirotic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/nikola-mirotic/000796/), [Donatas Motiejunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/donatas-motiejunas/lcz/), [Dario Saric](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/saric-dario/003112/), [Jonas Valanciunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/jonas-valanciunas/000898/), [Omri Casspi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/omri-casspi/jfo/), [Luigi Datome](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/luigi-datome/ccd/), and [Goga Bitadze](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/goga-bitadze/006809/), among others. The 2025-26 season aims to add new names to that list as Europe’s next generation steps into the spotlight.
