heroMedia.title: NextGen Ulm: Players to watch
heroMedia.summary: Some will be competing in their first NextGen tournament, while others will arrive in Ulm with more experience under their belt

content.text:
The first stop of the Adidas NextGen EuroLeague will bring some of the best young players from across the continent to compete for bragging rights at the [NextGen EuroLeague Ulm tournament](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/nge/news/nextgen-euroleague-tips-off-in-ulm-with-2022-champ-mega-2025-finalist-milan/). Every team features intriguing talent, and we asked the clubs to identify their player to watch.

Here are the eight players to watch at the NextGen Ulm event.

### Nikola Karalic (U18 Mega Super Belgrade)

[Nikola Karalic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/nikola-karalic/012906/) will be playing his third NextGen EuroLeague season, having helped Mega reach the NextGen Finals twice. Last season, he averaged 14.0 points at the NextGen Finals in Dubai after posting 7.5 points, 3.5 rebounds, 2.2 assists, and 1.8 steals at the NextGen Istanbul Qualifier. He also contributed to Mega winning the U19 ABA League title, his second championship in the competition.

This season, the 2.02-meter forward is averaging 8.9 points, 4.1 rebounds, and 2.4 assists for Mega’s sister club, OKK Belgrade, in the Serbian League, while also competing in the U19 ABA League. Gaining senior-level experience has been crucial for Karalic’s growth both on and off the court. A versatile player, he is a true three-level scorer and an elite defender thanks to his long wingspan. He can effectively play positions 2 through 4 and have a major impact on the game.

### Cameron Houindo (U18 Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana)

[Cameron Houindo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/cameron-houindo/013063/) will be playing in his third NextGen season. The big man moved from France to Ljubljana in the summer of 2025 after impressing at the 2024–25 NextGen tournament in Belgrade, where he averaged 12.0 points and 5.0 rebounds. He followed that with 9.7 points and 5.3 rebounds at the NextGen Finals in Abu Dhabi with U18 Pole France INSEP Paris.

The 2.06-meter center was named MVP of the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024, where France won the title and he averaged 13.9 points, 7.9 rebounds, 2.3 steals, and 2.3 blocks. Last summer, he posted 7.6 points, 5.0 rebounds, and 1.4 blocks at the FIBA U18 EuroBasket 2025.

This season, Houindo has played for Cedevita’s senior team, averaging 2.6 points in the EuroCup and 3.6 points in the Adriatic League, though he has been sidelined since mid-December with a hand/wrist injury. An excellent athlete, Houindo is effective in pick-and-roll situations and consistently active on the offensive glass. Improving his shooting efficiency and face-up game from the perimeter remains the next step in his development.

content.title: Is Cameron Houindo the next EuroCup star? | NextGen Highlights

content.text:
### Lucas Sanchez (U18 BAXI Manresa)

[Lucas Sanchez](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/lucas-sanchez/013499/) will be playing his second season in the NextGen competition after averaging 8.0 points, 4.3 rebounds, 2.5 assists, and 1.3 steals for Manresa at the NextGen Istanbul event last season. He has made one appearance for the club in both the Spanish League and the EuroCup.

Primarily competing in Spain’s U22 league, Sanchez is averaging 10.0 points, 3.9 rebounds, 2.9 assists, and 1.6 steals. He has good size for a point guard and a high basketball IQ. Known for his advanced pick-and-roll game, he can both facilitate for teammates and finish creatively in the paint. Continued improvement in his shooting and on-ball defense will be key to his progression.

### Jamie Edoka (U18 Next Gen Ulm Team)

[Jamie Edoka](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/jamie-edoka/012916/) is competing in the NextGen tournament for the second time after totaling 1 point and 1 rebound in 13 minutes over four games with the Next Gen Belgrade team in 2023–24. The German guard plays for Frankfurt and is averaging 16.1 points, 3.9 rebounds, and 2.4 assists in the ProB third division, along with 20.4 points, 4.8 rebounds, and 5.2 assists in the U19 NBBL.

Edoka has also made one appearance in the German League this season after four games at that level in 2024–25. On the international stage, he competed at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024 and later averaged 10.7 points, 4.0 rebounds, and 1.7 assists at the FIBA U18 EuroBasket 2025. A strong-bodied guard, Edoka can score in multiple ways, using his strength and athleticism offensively while standing out defensively for his versatility and instincts.

### Teo Milicic (U18 ratiopharm ulm)

[Teo Milicic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/teo-milicic/013624/) will be playing in his second NextGen event after averaging 6.8 points, 3.8 rebounds, 4.5 assists, and 1.2 steals in Ulm last season. This year, the point guard has averaged 1.9 points and 1.6 assists in the EuroCup, 1.7 points in the German League, 12.3 points, 4.8 rebounds, and 3.8 assists in ProB, and 16.3 points, 5.3 rebounds, and 4.3 assists in the U19 NBBL.

Basketball runs in the family: his father, Igor Milicic, is the head coach of the Polish national team and EuroCup club Aris Thessaloniki, while his brother, Igor Milicic Jr., plays in the NBA G League and is a key contributor for Poland. Teo is a highly competitive point guard with exceptional basketball IQ, playing with maturity beyond his years. A natural leader, he is constantly vocal on the floor and embraces responsibility in key moments. He can create his own shot, run the pick-and-roll efficiently, and score in multiple ways, while defensively setting the tone with relentless effort. Improving shot consistency remains a priority.

content.title: Teo Milicic in action for ratiopharm ulm's first team in the BKT EuroCup this season.

content.text:
### Ilias Chaidemenos (U18 Olympiacos Piraeus)

[Ilias Chaidemenos](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/ilias-chaidemenos/014308/) is making his NextGen debut. He is averaging 13.4 points, 4.1 rebounds, 3.8 assists, and 1.6 steals in the Rising Stars U18 League. He was part of the Greece squad that finished fourth at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024, where he posted 12.0 points, 3.4 rebounds, 1.6 assists, and 1.4 steals.

Previously with Peristeri, Chaidemenos is an athletic guard with a strong scorer’s instinct. He is particularly effective in one-on-one situations, combining speed, control, and confidence with the ball. He also shows leadership, solid decision-making, and the ability to create advantages for both himself and his teammates.

### Luca Bandirali (U18 EA7 Emporio Armani Milan)

[Luca Bandirali](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/luca-bandirali/014268/) will be making his NextGen debut. He is averaging 9.4 points in Serie B, 8.5 points in the U19 league, and 8.6 points with 2.9 rebounds in the NextGen Cup. He joined Milan from Cantu this past offseason.

The 2.01-meter forward represented Italy for two summers at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket. He was part of the team that finished second in 2023, qualifying for the FIBA U17 Basketball World Cup 2024, and later averaged 4.0 points and 3.0 rebounds at the 2024 U16 European Championship. An explosive athlete with good size, Bandirali can attack closeouts, finish above the rim, and use his strength to score inside. Defensively, he is effective guarding multiple positions thanks to his mobility, length, and help-side instincts.

### Marvyn Wade (U18 LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne)

[Marvyn Wade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/nge/players/marvyn-wade/013016/) will be playing his third season in the NextGen competition after averaging 11.8 points, 4.8 rebounds, 3.2 assists, and 1.5 steals in Ulm last year. This season, he is posting 13.9 points, 2.9 rebounds, 5.5 assists, and 2.2 steals with ASVEL’s U21 team.

At the national level, Wade helped France win the title at the FIBA U16 EuroBasket 2024, contributing 5.1 points per game. He stands out as a dynamic scorer with a natural attacking mindset. His speed allows him to consistently beat defenders, create space, and push the pace, especially in transition. His feel for the game is a major strength, though continued work on emotional control and frustration management will aid his development.

heroMedia.title: adidas and Euroleague Basketball renew partnership to empower the next generation of talent

content.text:
Euroleague Basketball and adidas today announced the renewal of their long-standing partnership for the next four seasons, through the 2028–29 campaign. Building on more than a decade of collaboration since first joining forces in 2014, the renewed agreement highlights adidas’ position as a driving force in European basketball and deepens its commitment to shaping the sport’s future across both professional and grassroots levels.

As the naming partner of the [adidas NextGen EuroLeague](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/ngt/), adidas will continue to play a pivotal role in developing Europe’s rising stars, reinforcing a shared vision with Euroleague Basketball to grow the game from the grassroots up. The competition has become the benchmark for showcasing future talent, perfectly aligned with adidas’ dedication to creating opportunities for the next generation of players. Since 2022, adidas has further elevated its presence by introducing adidas-branded teams at each Qualifier, bringing together some of the most promising prospects from across Europe.

"adidas has been a fundamental partner in growing Euroleague Basketball for more than a decade, and together we have elevated both the professional and grassroots levels of basketball," said Gawain Davies, Euroleague Basketball Chief Commercial Officer. "Their commitment to youth development through the adidas NextGen EuroLeague competition, together with their support across the EuroLeague, makes this collaboration unique. We are excited to continue this journey with adidas through the 2028–29 season."

“At adidas, we believe the future of basketball belongs to the next generation,” said Cameron Mason, Senior Director of Sports Marketing Basketball at adidas. “Our partnership with Euroleague allows us to not only invest in young athletes but also celebrate the culture and rivalries that make the game so powerful. Together, we are creating moments that inspire players and fans alike across Europe.”

Beyond youth development, the renewed agreement will also spotlight initiatives that bring fans closer to the game, including the **Rivalry Series**, which elevates one key EuroLeague fixture each round by celebrating historic clashes and defining moments of the season across social, digital, and broadcast platforms, and continued adidas support of the **EuroLeague Final Four**, the pinnacle event of European club basketball.

Together, Euroleague Basketball and adidas reaffirm their shared commitment to growing the game from the grassroots up, inspiring the next generation of talent and amplifying the energy of European basketball culture.

heroMedia.title: Dominykas Grunkis takes home MVP trophy after ending Zalgiris's title drought
heroMedia.summary: He grabs the top honor after Zalgiris win first NextGen crown since 2007

linkedIds.text: U18 EA7 Emporio Armani Milan v U18 Zalgiris Kaunas (2025-05-25T09:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Dominykas Grunkis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/ngt/players/dominykas-grunkis/013621/) etched his name in the annals of Adidas NextGen EuroLeague Finals history by claiming the Most Valuable Trophy as [U18 Zalgiris Kaunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/ngt/teams/u18-zalgiris-kaunas/jza/) ended an 18-year doubt to capture the club’s third title in the illustrious competition. 

Grunkis scored 11 points, grabbed 2 rebounds and had 7 assists in [Zalgiris’s 89-81 victory over U18 EA7 Emporio Armani Milan](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/ngt/news/jtf424-next-gen-dubai-report-final-milan-zalgiris/) in the NextGen Championship Game. The Zalgiris guard was joined on the All-Tournament Team by the Milan duo of Luigo Suigo and Achille Lonati, Ognjen Srzentic of U18 Mega Super Belgrade, and U18 Real Madrid's Sidi Gueye. 

For the tournament, Grunkis, who was only four months old when Zalgiris celebrated title number two in 2007, averaged 13.2 points on 53 percent three-point shooting, 2.2 rebounds and 3.5 assists for a performance index rating average of 14.2. His biggest performance came in the team’s opening game – an overtime win over U18 Pole France INSEP Paris in which he scored 28 points with 6 three-pointers. Grunkis did not finish in the top 10 of any statistical category. 

Suigo, for his part, collected 21 points, 11 rebounds and 4 assists for a PIR of 33 in Milan’s first-ever Championship Game. He finished with 14.5 points, 7.5 rebounds, 1.5 assists, 1.2 steals and 2.2 blocks for an average efficiency of 21.5. Suigo ranked second in PIR, seventh in points and fourth in rebounds. 

Lonati poured in 26 points with 2 rebounds, 3 assists, 3 steals and 1 block for a 22 PIR in the Championship Game. The guard averaged 23.8 points with 48 percent shooting, 4.8 rebounds, 4.2 assists, 1.8 steals and 1.0 blocks for a PIR average of 22.8. He was fourth in PIR, first in scoring, eighth in assists and second in steals. 

Gueye tried all he could to lead Real to a third straight crown, but it wasn’t enough. The center tallied 17.3 points, 8.7 rebounds and 1.7 blocks for a tournament-best 24.7 PIR. Gueye also was sixth in scoring and fourth in rebounds. 

The other All-Tournament Team member was Srzentic, who tallied 22.0 points, 5.0 rebounds, 3.3 assists and 1.7 steals for a PIR of 20.7. He ranked sixth in PIR and second in points.

heroMedia.title: U18 Barcelona storms into first-place game
heroMedia.summary: Barcelona forced 26 turnovers and outrebounded Trento 51-28

linkedIds.text: U18 FC Barcelona v U18 Dolomiti Energia Trento (2026-03-14T14:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
U18 FC Barcelona thrashed U18 Dolomiti Energia Trento 92-50 to win Group A at the adidas NextGen EuroLeague qualifying tournament in Bologna. It was Barcelona's third win in as many games, third by at least 22 points and second by a margin of at least 40 points, and a victory that earned it a spot in Sunday's first-place games. Trento finished group stage with a 2-1 record and will feature in the third-place contest.

Nikola Kusturica led Barcelona with 21 points, 8 rebounds and 4 steals. Jakob Siftar and Joaquim Boumtje Boumtje each scored 13, while Abdrahamane Kone scored 12 and Mohamed Dabone 11 points in a big win. Giulio Vergnaghi led Trento with 20 points and Markuss Jahovics had 11 in a loss.

heroMedia.title: U18 Bayern holds off U18 Paris, 75-82
heroMedia.summary: Bayern wins a clash of two previously winless teams

linkedIds.text: U18 Paris Basketball v U18 FC Bayern Munich (2026-03-14T12:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
Andrija Sucic had 22 points and 8 rebounds to lead U18 FC Bayern Munich past U18 Paris Basketball 75-82 to claim its first win at the adidas NextGen EuroLeague qualifying tournament in Bologna. Bayern finished Group A with a 1-2 record and will play in a fifth-place game on Sunday, while Paris is 0-3 and will play for seventh on Sunday.

In addition to Sucic, Bayern had three more scorers in double figures. Caspar Vossenberg scored 17 points, Namik Muratovic had 14, while Kilian Dueck had 10 points and 14 rebounds. For Paris, Mayles Barry scored 15 points, while Dario Cokara, Mamadou Sissoko and Melvyn Vounang Nanfah scored 13 apiece in a loss.

heroMedia.title: U18 Baskonia holds off U18 Virtus to get to 2-0
heroMedia.summary: Baskonia will play against Next Gen Team later on Saturday for a place in the first-place game

linkedIds.text: U18 Fundacion Baskonia-Alaves v U18 Virtus Bologna (2026-03-14T10:15:00.000Z)

content.text:
U18 Fundacion Baskonia-Alaves picked up its second win of the adidas NextGen EuroLeague qualifying tournament in Bologna by holding off the home side U18 Virtus Bologna 69-65. Baskonia improved to 2-0 in Group B, while Virtus lost another dramatic game, dropping to 0-2.

Baskonia took he lead for good in the first quarter, and had control of the game, leading by as many as 14 points in the second quarter. But the hosts kept staying close enough, and got within 69-65 with over 2 minutes to go after a three-pointer by Simone Ventura. But that turned out to be the last basket of the game, as Baskonia prevailed.

Mate Khatiashvili had 19 points in the winning effort, Chiek Diallo had 16 points, Juom Maker Bol collected 11 points plus 9 rebounds, and Stefan Joksimovic also scored 11 for Baskonia. Livio Berlingeri scored 18, Ventura had 17 for Virtus, while Thomas Acunzo had 10 points plus 11 rebounds.
